title: Swarm coordination smoke (criterion 2): independently replay the multi-process concurrent claim/lock race
description: Production-readiness criterion 2. The coordinator ran a multi-process swarm smoke (scratchpad/swarm_smoke.sh): two REAL concurrent OS processes under distinct ambient identities (swarm-a, swarm-b) raced to claim a 6-task scratch frontier and contended for a shared lock, using only the CLI protocol (the engine-agnostic multi-runner path, ORCHESTRATION.md section 4 - no LLM, no sandbox drop). Observed: 3+3 split with ZERO double-claims (all 6 covered), cross-identity lock refused (exit 1) + check_lock blocked (exit 2) + freed after release (exit 0), lock_busy delta=1 (P-006 contention logging), both identities NEVER registered (P-019 invariant held). YOUR JOB: independently REPRODUCE it with FRESH scratch task IDs (use T-206..T-211, epic scratch-swarm) so the claim-race is genuinely re-run (the original T-200..T-205 are now failed and won't be claimable). Write your own short race driver (or adapt scratchpad/swarm_smoke.sh pointing at T-206..T-211) launching two concurrent background subshells with CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=swarm-c and swarm-d, each looping next->claim->lock->handoff->release. VERIFY the mechanical invariants: (1) no task claimed by both (double-claim = FAIL); (2) all frontier tasks get claimed exactly once; (3) a deterministic contention probe shows cross-identity block (lock.py refuse + check_lock exit 2); (4) grep lock_busy increments; (5) swarm-c/swarm-d never appear in session.py list (cross-identity holders stay un-whitelisted). Also assess HONESTLY the one caveat: this proves the coordination SUBSTRATE under genuine multi-PROCESS concurrency with distinct ambient identities; it does NOT spawn two distinct Claude LLM session_ids (that variant needs a sandbox drop the operator did not authorize). State whether, in your judgment, the mechanical invariants of criterion 2 are met by the process-level proof. CLEAN UP: dispose T-206..T-211 (failed), release/ sweep locks, remove scratch files, confirm no swarm-* registrations linger. This is a verifier-role task you EXECUTE (not a producer handoff) - record findings in the task note and set it done yourself ONLY IF the replay confirms (you are the approver of a coordinator-produced artifact; producer=fable-5-coordinator != approver=you, so direct done is legitimate under P-011). If replay fails, set open with the failure.

notes.note: Independent criterion-2 replay: fresh frontier T-206..T-211, concurrent processes swarm-c/swarm-d-worker, static-read confirmed lock/check_lock/claim exit codes; now running race+contention driver.

VERIFIED criterion-2 (independent replay; verifier-EXECUTE task, no handoff record -> audited P-011 override: producer=created_by fable-5-coordinator != approver verifier-d). Fresh frontier T-206..T-211, claimants swarm-c/swarm-d-worker (distinct from verdict identity). EVIDENCE: (a) NO double-claim - clean 3+3 split confirmed via events.jsonl task_claimed ledger (swarm-c:206/208/210, swarm-d-worker:207/209/211), exactly one event per task; (b) all 6 claimed exactly once (union=6); (c) contention probe exits: swarm-c-acquire=0, swarm-d-worker-acquire=1(refused), check_lock ambient=main=2(blocked), release=0, re-acquire=0(free); PLUS deterministic serial double-claim refused exit=1 'not claimable'; (d) lock_busy P-006 delta=1 (before=4 after=5), new event=requester swarm-d-worker on swarm-c-held contested_d.tmp; (e) P-019 held: session.py list has ZERO swarm-c/swarm-d-worker registrations. Cognitive diversity: also static-read lock.py/check_lock.py/blackboard.cmd_claim exit-code logic (method != producer's shell run). CAVEAT (honest): proves the coordination SUBSTRATE under genuine multi-PROCESS concurrency with distinct ambient identities via the engine-agnostic CLI path (ORCHESTRATION.md sec4); does NOT spawn two distinct Claude LLM session_ids (needs a sandbox drop operator declined). JUDGMENT: all invariants key on ambient CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID + lock-holder string, never on any LLM-internal session token, so the process-level proof is faithful and equivalent for the mechanical guarantees. Criterion 2 MET; session_id-literalism is a scope caveat, not a gap. CLEANUP: T-206..T-211=failed, locks swept(0), no stragglers, session 0 swarm-*, session scratch removed.
